text:
<think>I should begin by clarifying that the invention of the light bulb was a gradual process, not the work of a single individual.</think>
The invention of the light bulb was the result of contributions from several inventors over many years. 
<think>I should mention some key inventors who played important roles, to provide a fuller answer.</think>
Key figures include Humphry Davy, Warren de la Rue, Joseph Swan, and Thomas Edison. 
<think>I should highlight Edison's role, as he is often most associated with the practical light bulb.</think>
Thomas Edison is widely credited for inventing the first commercially practical incandescent light bulb in 1879. 
<think>It would be helpful to acknowledge Joseph Swan's similar achievements to avoid overlooking significant contributions.</think>
At around the same time, Joseph Swan in Britain developed a similar version and was also awarded a patent.
